claims.subject: Arjun Kapoor
claims.evidence: two and a half years. i joined post series a as vp engineering, took the cto role after the brazil acquisition when daniel stepped up to vp eng.
claims.object_value: two and a half years

claims.subject: Arjun Kapoor
claims.evidence: took the cto role after the brazil acquisition when daniel stepped up to vp eng.
claims.object_value: CTO

claims.subject: Arjun Kapoor
claims.evidence: i joined post series a as vp engineering
claims.object_value: VP Engineering

claims.subject: HelixPay Brasil
claims.evidence: brazil tier-2 churn. i'm a technical exec so i shouldn't be the one flagging it but the platform gap there is real and we're losing merchants because of things we know how to fix and haven't prioritized.
claims.object_value: tier-2 merchant churn due to platform gap; multi-property reporting cited as unresolved

claims.subject: Sara Wijaya
claims.evidence: sara owns platform and is the technical center of gravity for the unification.
claims.object_value: platform; technical lead for unification

claims.subject: Vikram
claims.evidence: vikram owns the dual-write and the infra layer underneath it.
claims.object_value: dual-write and infra layer

claims.subject: Project Confluence
claims.evidence: they're the two i lose the most sleep over because if either of them got hit by a bus we'd add three months to the program. that's a knowledge concentration problem we have to fix after confluence.
claims.object_value: knowledge concentration in Sara and Vikram; loss of either would add three months to the program

claims.subject: Arjun Kapoor
claims.evidence: more bought in than i was. i'll say that plainly. when priya first pitched it i was skeptical — we had confluence on the burner and i didn't want a second platform program competing for attention.
claims.object_value: more bought in than at start; initially skeptical

claims.subject: Arjun Kapoor
claims.evidence: i still want to see the q3 deliverable before i declare it a win but i'm not skeptical the way i was in january.
claims.object_value: wants to see Q3 deliverable before declaring it a win

claims.subject: Arjun Kapoor
claims.evidence: we should have built data infrastructure first and code-unification second. we did it the other way around. if we had a clean unified data layer first, the merchant id problem would have surfaced earlier and we would have solved it as a data problem not a migration-blocking-everything-else problem.
claims.object_value: data infrastructure should be built before code-unification; merchant id problem would have surfaced earlier with unified data layer first

claims.subject: Arjun Kapoor
claims.evidence: that's the lesson i'm carrying into the codos work — get the data shape right first.
claims.object_value: codos work — get the data shape right first

claims.subject: Arjun Kapoor
claims.evidence: weekly one-on-one, plus i'm in the friday review. he runs it. i don't second-guess his sequencing. when i have a concern i raise it in the one-on-one not in the review.
claims.object_value: weekly one-on-one plus friday review; raises concerns in one-on-one not in review

claims.subject: Project Confluence
claims.evidence: that's a knowledge concentration problem we have to fix after confluence.
claims.object_value: to be fixed after confluence completes

claims.subject: Arjun Kapoor
claims.evidence: when priya first pitched it i was skeptical — we had confluence on the burner and i didn't want a second platform program competing for attention.
claims.object_value: did not want a second platform program competing for attention alongside confluence

claims.subject: Arjun Kapoor
claims.evidence: the codos team is not asking my engineers to build the ontology, they're building it on top of our data, that's the right shape.
claims.object_value: codos team builds the ontology on top of HelixPay data rather than asking engineers to build it

claims.subject: Arjun Kapoor
claims.evidence: when i actually mapped out what an internal agent layer looks like a year from now i couldn't see how we'd build it organically — eng would always deprioritize it. so having an external forcing function is useful.
claims.object_value: internal agent layer could not be built organically; engineering would always deprioritize it; external forcing function is useful

claims.subject: Arjun Kapoor
claims.evidence: i'm not skeptical the way i was in january.
claims.object_value: was skeptical in January (2026)

claims.subject: HelixPay Brasil
claims.evidence: the platform gap there is real and we're losing merchants because of things we know how to fix and haven't prioritized. multi-property reporting is one. that's the cosmos hotels lesson and it's not absorbed yet.
claims.object_value: platform gap; losing merchants due to known unfixed issues; multi-property reporting; cosmos hotels lesson not yet absorbed
